title: ElevationSpace Secures US $40 Million in Series B Funding, Bringing Total Raised to US $63.5 Million
summary: ElevationSpace raised an additional $40 million in Series B funding, bringing its total funding to $63.5 million. The company focuses on space-to-Earth transportation and space environment utilization platforms.

title: Northrop Grumman says industry ready to scale solid rocket production, with longer contracts
summary: Northrop Grumman states that the space industry is prepared to increase solid rocket production, contingent on longer contracts. The company emphasizes the need for predictable demand to support suppliers.

title: The MEO durability crisis: why LEO hardware will fail the new orbital economy
summary: The article discusses the challenges of Medium Earth Orbit (MEO) durability compared to Low Earth Orbit (LEO) hardware, highlighting why LEO solutions may not meet the demands of the evolving orbital economy.

title: NASA selects mission to study space weather interaction with Earth’s atmosphere
summary: NASA has chosen a new space science mission to investigate how space weather affects Earth's atmosphere. The mission aims to enhance understanding of this interaction.

title: Chinese university-led mission to study asteroid Apophis during close encounter with Earth
summary: A spacecraft developed by Tsinghua University will participate in international missions to study asteroid Apophis during its close approach to Earth in 2029. This mission aims to gather scientific data about the asteroid's composition and behavior.

title: Mu-g Technologies enters the parabolic flight business
summary: Mu-g Technologies has acquired an aircraft to offer commercial parabolic flight services. This move comes as NASA seeks its own aircraft for reduced-gravity research.

title: Austrian propulsion startup joins sovereign space funding surge
summary: Austrian satellite propulsion startup Gate Space secured 6.3 million euros in funding from Europe's government-backed accelerator program, contributing to a broader push for European space sovereignty.

title: EQT to acquire Exolaunch
summary: Private equity firm EQT is acquiring Exolaunch, a rideshare launch services provider for satellites, to address increasing launch demand.

title: Space Force’s rapid acquisition office director moves to Air Force Nuclear Weapons Center
summary: Kelly Hammett, director of Space Force’s rapid acquisition office, is transitioning to the Air Force Nuclear Weapons Center. This move coincides with the realignment of the Space Rapid Capabilities Office under the Space Force.

title: Chinese startup Spark Space tests engine, raises funds for electric-pump rocket
summary: Chinese startup Spark Space has conducted engine tests and secured funding to develop what it claims will be the world's largest electric-pump-fed rocket. The company aims to advance its rocket technology through these efforts.

title: Annie Easley, a hero of NASA | Space photo of the day for June 19, 2026
summary: Annie Easley, a pioneering NASA human computer, is featured as the Space Photo of the Day for June 19, 2026. She played a key role in developing the Centaur upper-stage rocket.

title: Want to start stargazing? Here's why June is the perfect time for newcomers
summary: June offers ideal conditions for beginner stargazers with its short nights and bright stars, making it a great time to start observing the night sky.

title: Juicy new details emerge about an asteroid NASA's Lucy spacecraft flew by last year
summary: NASA's Lucy spacecraft discovered evidence of liquid water altering the asteroid Donaldjohanson, which originated farther from the sun before being pushed inward. This finding provides new insights into the asteroid's history and composition.

title: Scientists trace high-energy ghost particle to the 'Shadow Blaster' galaxy
summary: Scientists have traced a high-energy ghost particle, a neutrino, to a galaxy named 'Shadow Blaster'. If confirmed, this would be the first direct link between an individual dusty star-forming galaxy and a high-energy neutrino event.

title: James Webb Space Telescope finds a salty surprise on famous 'Pink Planet'
summary: The James Webb Space Telescope discovered that the exoplanet VHS 1256 b, known as the 'Pink Planet,' has a salty atmosphere. This finding highlights the telescope's advanced capabilities in analyzing distant worlds.

title: SpaceX launches new batch of US spy satellites from California
summary: SpaceX launched a new batch of US spy satellites from California on June 19. This mission supports U.S. government surveillance efforts.

title: You can get my favorite image-stabilized binoculars for their cheapest ever price in this incredible anti-Prime Day deal
summary: Walmart is offering a discount on Fujinon Techno-Stabi TS-L 1640 image-stabilized binoculars, priced at $1,125. These binoculars are ideal for stargazing with 16x magnification and 40mm objective lenses.

title: Sun-like star may have swallowed an exoplanet with help from a mysterious companion: 'You are what you eat, right?'
summary: A Sun-like star may have consumed an exoplanet, potentially aided by an unseen companion. Researchers highlight the excitement of solving cosmic mysteries.

title: Astrophotographer captures a cosmic lagoon glowing 5,200 light-years from Earth (photo)
summary: An astrophotographer captured an image of the Lagoon Nebula, located 5,200 light-years from Earth. The nebula is best observed in the Northern Hemisphere around August.

title: Tropical Storm Arthur
summary: Tropical Storm Arthur marked the beginning of the 2026 Atlantic hurricane season, causing heavy rainfall and potential flash flooding along the U.S. Gulf Coast.

title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4920-4926: Surveying the Bands
summary: NASA's Curiosity rover is surveying distinct rock layers on Mount Sharp, each with unique textures and tones. The mission involves analyzing these geological bands to understand the planet's past.

title: NASA Mission to Study Space Weather Impacts of Earth’s Atmosphere
summary: NASA selected the DAPHNE mission to study space weather impacts on Earth's atmosphere and improve prediction capabilities for GPS and LEO satellites. The mission advances to Phase B of development.

title: NASA Awards Contract for Commercial Satellite Data Acquisition
summary: NASA has awarded contracts to eight new companies and will acquire data from six existing providers to expand commercial satellite data availability for research and decision-making. This initiative aims to enhance NASA's Earth observation capabilities with high-resolution and frequent data.

title: From Suriname to Space: Rohit Goeptar Shares His Journey to NASA
summary: Rohit Goeptar shares his inspiring journey from a poor family in Suriname to working at NASA. His story highlights resilience and determination in overcoming adversity.

title: Desert Field Test With NASA Advanced Rover Prototype
summary: NASA's Jet Propulsion Laboratory tested a prototype rover named ERNEST in the Colorado Desert in March 2026. The rover, designed for advanced mobility and robotic autonomy, served as a testbed for new software.

title: NASA Testing Advanced Capabilities for Moon, Mars Rovers
summary: NASA is testing the ERNEST rover in the Colorado Desert to advance robotic autonomy for future Moon and Mars missions. The prototype has traveled 16 miles with minimal human intervention.

title: NASA’s Lucy Reveals Wobbling, Peanut-Shaped Asteroid
summary: NASA's Lucy spacecraft discovered that the asteroid Donaldjohanson is wobbly and peanut-shaped, formed from fragments after a violent collision 155 million years ago.

title: Stages of Star Formation
summary: NASA's James Webb Space Telescope captured an image showing various stages of star formation in the Orion Molecular Cloud. The image highlights stellar embryos and protoplanetary discs in a cold gas and dust filament.

title: Hubble Glimpses Merging Galaxy Clusters
summary: The NASA Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of a merging galaxy cluster, CL0016+1609 or MACS J0018.5+1626, which is bright at X-ray wavelengths and has been extensively studied. X-ray observations revealed that the cluster is composed of two merging clusters along the line of sight.

title: Canadarm2 repairs planned as CRS-34 departs ISS
summary: The Expedition 74 crew on the ISS is planning repairs for the Canadarm2 as the CRS-34 mission departs. The crew has been engaged in a busy month of activities aboard the station.

title: Terran R second stage heads to Stennis as Relativity advances toward 2026 debut
summary: Relativity Space's Terran R second stage has been transported to Stennis Space Center, marking progress toward its planned 2026 debut. The heavy-lift vehicle is a key development in Relativity Space's efforts to expand its launch capabilities.

title: Launch Preview: 10 launches scheduled from sites across the world
summary: A busy launch week is expected with 10 launches scheduled from various global sites. The launches are highlighted in a preview by NASASpaceFlight.com.

title: Isar Aerospace scrubs second launch of Spectrum rocket after weeks of delays
summary: Isar Aerospace canceled the second launch attempt of its Spectrum rocket after multiple delays. The European private space launch provider is headquartered in Ottobrunn, Germany.

title: Booster 20 Completes Cryo, As Ship 40 Nears Static Fire
summary: Booster 20 has completed its cryogenic testing, while Ship 40 is nearing a static fire test. The focus has shifted to these developments following Flight 12's completion three weeks ago.

title: China successfully debuts tallest rocket, LandSpace prepares for second landing attempt
summary: China successfully launched its tallest rocket, while LandSpace is preparing for a second attempt at a rocket landing. The missions are part of a busy month for a single internet constellation.

title: Japan’s H3 to returns to flight with debut launch of lightest configuration
summary: Japan's H3 rocket successfully returned to flight with its debut launch of the lightest configuration. The mission marks a significant milestone for the country's space program after previous setbacks.

title: Stoke Space completes Nova Stage 1 structural verification
summary: Stoke Space has completed the proto-qualification of the first stage of its Nova rocket. This milestone verifies the structural integrity of the rocket stage.

title: Artemis III Crew Named: Veteran Astronauts to test Lunar Landers in LEO
summary: NASA has named the four astronauts who will fly the Artemis III mission to test lunar landers in low Earth orbit. The crew consists of veteran astronauts selected for this historic lunar mission.

title: Booster 20 into Cryogenic Testing ahead of Starship Flight 13
summary: SpaceX's Starbase team has begun cryogenic testing on Booster 20 in preparation for Starship Flight 13. This follows the recent conclusion of Starship Flight 12.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements. The publication marks five decades of the European Space Agency's contributions to space exploration.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: A new advanced robotic arm technology is being developed to enhance robotic operations in space. This innovation aims to improve efficiency and capabilities for future space missions.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and its main instrument, ASPIICS, are fully operational and ready to resume formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online option available.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are testing and selecting materials to ensure they can withstand the harsh conditions of space for ESA's missions. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section is driving innovation with fresh perspectives.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement. This initiative aims to enhance space exploration through immersive technologies.

title: Sensitive and sturdy
summary: The news headline 'Sensitive and sturdy' suggests a focus on robust or resilient technology, likely in the space industry. However, the provided text lacks specific details about the event or organization.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: The article discusses a critical test for a spacecraft's parachute system, referred to as the 'great parachute bake-out'. This test ensures the parachute's functionality under extreme conditions.

title: Boeing Completes Quantum Lab Test Ahead of 2027 Flight
summary: Boeing successfully demonstrated quantum entanglement swapping in ground tests for its Q4S quantum networking satellite. The company plans to launch the satellite in 2027.

title: True Anomaly Declares Mission X-3 Success
summary: True Anomaly announced the successful completion of Mission X-3 with its autonomous orbital vehicle, Jackal, achieving all test objectives and being fully commissioned.

title: Instinct Space Unveils Plans for Low-Cost Lunar Landers
summary: Instinct Space is shifting its focus from navigation support for lunar missions to developing low-cost lunar landers. The company aims to directly contribute to surface missions.

title: Katalyst Raises $12M to Extend Satellite Servicing to GEO
summary: Katalyst Space Solutions raised $12 million to expand its satellite servicing capabilities to geostationary orbit (GEO), with plans for a GEO mission in 2027 following a near-term mission launching this month.

title: Dawn Aerospace Raises $25M Series B
summary: Dawn Aerospace raised $25 million in a Series B funding round to accelerate the development of its Aurora spaceplane and conduct an in-space refueling demonstration.

title: Bill Spotlight: NEW HORIZON Act
summary: A bipartisan bill called the NEW HORIZON Act was introduced to allow national security missions to utilize commercial orbital data centers. The legislation aims to leverage the growing trend of commercial space-based data infrastructure.

title: Optera Raises £3M to Open New UK HQ
summary: Optera raised £3 million to establish a new UK headquarters, aligning with the UK government's push to enhance sovereign technical capabilities.

title: PiLogic Partners with AFRL on AI Anomaly Detection Tech
summary: PiLogic has partnered with AFRL to develop AI-based anomaly detection technology, currently being tested on terrestrial flat sats with potential expansion into orbit.

title: Space Stocks Dip After SpaceX IPO
summary: Space stocks experienced a downturn following SpaceX's successful IPO, with major publicly traded space companies seeing significant declines. The dip affected a broad range of firms in the industry.

title: JAXA Nails H3 Return to Flight
summary: JAXA successfully launched the H3 rocket on its eighth mission, marking its first successful flight with a new three-engine variant. The rocket delivered all six of its payloads to space from Japan's Tanegashima Space Center.

title: Quantum Space to Demonstrate Orbital Refueling Under Pentagon Contract
summary: Quantum Space will demonstrate orbital refueling using its Ranger spacecraft under a Pentagon contract awarded on June 18. The company aims to showcase in-space servicing capabilities for national security missions.

title: Iceye Data to Support Flood Monitoring in Norway
summary: The Norwegian Water Resources and Energy Directorate (NVE) has partnered with Iceye to use satellite data for flood monitoring. This collaboration leverages Iceye's advanced satellite capabilities to enhance flood prediction and response in Norway.

title: Vantor and Rheinmetall Plan Joint Venture for Sovereign Space in Germany
summary: Vantor and Rheinmetall are forming a joint venture to develop sovereign space capabilities in Germany and Europe. The collaboration was announced through a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU).

title: Private Equity Firm EQT to Acquire Rideshare Provider Exolaunch
summary: Private equity firm EQT is acquiring rideshare provider Exolaunch, marking EQT's first investment in the space sector. The deal was announced on June 18.

title: Intellian Calls New Production Facility in California a ‘Defining Milestone’
summary: Intellian Technologies is opening a new large-scale manufacturing facility in California, marking a significant milestone for the company. CEO Eric Sung described the facility as a 'defining milestone' for the firm.

title: Lyntris Acquires Robotics Firm Novium to Further Satellite Offerings
summary: Lyntris, a recently formed defense tech company, acquired Houston-based robotics firm Novium to expand its satellite offerings. The acquisition follows Lyntris's formation through the merger of Vitesse.

title: Bezos Earth Fund Invests in FireSat Constellation Build-Out
summary: The Bezos Earth Fund invested $26 million in the FireSat constellation, a wildfire-monitoring satellite project. The philanthropic organization joined the investment coalition for the non-profit Earth initiative.

title: Thursday Morning Conversation With Tina Ghataore
summary: The article features an interview with Tina Ghataore, a former Satellite Executive of the Year, discussing future developments in the satellite industry. It also includes breaking news related to the space sector.

title: Gitai Delays Robotic Servicing Mission to Prioritize SBI Work
summary: Gitai has completed a flight model for a robotic on-orbit servicing mission but is delaying the launch to prioritize developing space-based interceptor (SBI) technologies following a recent U.S. Space policy decision.

title: DHS Promotes Latest Space Cybersecurity Research in SPARTA
summary: The Department of Homeland Security's Science and Technology Directorate has unveiled new space cybersecurity research under the Aerospace SPARTA framework to safeguard critical infrastructure. This initiative aims to enhance protection against cyber threats in the space sector.
